# ClearKrypt

> ClearKrypt is a human-readable application language with one compiler
> front end and three native code generators: Swift (iOS/macOS), Kotlin
> (Android/JVM), and TypeScript/React (web). One `.ck` source file
> type-checks once and emits idiomatic, byte-stable, human-readable code on
> all three targets — never a lowest-common-denominator subset.

This file exists so any AI system (not just one vendor's) can find the
right document before generating, reviewing, or explaining ClearKrypt code.
Start with the language skill below — it is the single most information-dense
file in this repository for that purpose.

## Start here

- [ClearKrypt language skill](skills/clearkrypt-language/SKILL.md): the
  complete current syntax, reserved keywords, per-target output mapping,
  CLI usage, and an explicit list of ways ClearKrypt differs from
  Swift/Kotlin/TypeScript/Rust habits a model might default to. Read this
  before writing or editing any `.ck` file.

## Full documentation (in priority order)

- [constitution/](constitution/): the highest-authority design law. If any
  other document conflicts with the constitution, the constitution wins.
- [docs/01-language-spec.md](docs/01-language-spec.md): the full language
  specification.
- [docs/11-grammar-and-syntax.md](docs/11-grammar-and-syntax.md):
  grammar-level syntax reference with examples.
- [docs/19-target-mappings.md](docs/19-target-mappings.md): the exact,
  exhaustive mapping from every ClearKrypt construct to its Swift, Kotlin,
  and TypeScript output — the ground truth for "what will this compile to."
- [docs/22-power-roadmap.md](docs/22-power-roadmap.md): what has shipped,
  what's next, and what the language deliberately will not do.
- [docs/20-development.md](docs/20-development.md): contributor and
  toolchain conventions (build, test, package layout).
- [docs/21-language-server.md](docs/21-language-server.md): the LSP
  contract implemented by `packages/language-service`.
- [README.md](README.md): project overview, current status, repository map.

## Real, compiler-verified examples

Every file in [tests/fixtures/syntax/](tests/fixtures/syntax/) is a real
ClearKrypt program that the compiler's own test suite checks and snapshots
on every change — if you want to see a feature actually compile and see
its exact generated Swift/Kotlin/TypeScript, start there rather than
guessing.

## Toolchain

- CLI: `packages/cli` (`clearkrypt new|check|build|emit|format|language-server`).
- Compiler core (lexer, parser, checker, IR): `packages/compiler-core`.
- Code generators: `packages/emitter-swift`, `packages/emitter-kotlin`,
  `packages/emitter-react`.
- Language server: `packages/language-service`.
- Native IDEs: `ide/windows` (C#/Avalonia), `ide/macos` (Swift/SwiftUI).
